root_text.an6.80:1.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nacirasseva mahantattaṁ vepullattaṁ pāpuṇāti dhammesu.
root_text.an6.80:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ālokabahulo ca hoti yogabahulo ca vedabahulo ca asantuṭṭhibahulo ca anikkhittadhuro ca kusalesu dhammesu uttari ca patāreti.
root_text.an6.80:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nacirasseva mahantattaṁ vepullattaṁ pāpuṇāti dhammesū”ti.

translation_text.an6.80:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with six qualities soon acquires great and abundant good qualities.
translation_text.an6.80:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is full of light, full of practice, full of inspiration, and full of eagerness. They don’t slack off when it comes to developing skillful qualities. They reach further.
translation_text.an6.80:1.4: A mendicant who possesses these six qualities soon acquires great and abundant good qualities.”

variant_text.an6.80:1.3: uttari ca → uttariñca (bj, pts1ed) | patāreti → pakaroti (mr)
